spy_emails.email_body:
Hi there,

I was looking at Bluechip Real Estate's presence in Dubai and noticed your social media channels are pretty quiet right now.

For brokerages in the Dubai market, that's usually where warm buyers are scrolling before they ever pick up the phone.

I put together 3 Instagram post concepts specifically for Dubai — the kind of content that gets saves, shares, and DMs. I even designed mockups so you can see exactly how they'd look on your feed.

Want me to send them over? No charge, just want your read on them.

If they land, we can talk about what ongoing content support would look like for Bluechip Real Estate.

SaJib Shikder
NanoSoft Agency | nanosoft.agency

reverse_pitch.subject: quick question about Goji Labs's dev capacity
reverse_pitch.body:
Hi there,

I noticed Goji Labs is handling overflow — and I'm genuinely curious about something.

When you get more project requests than your small team can handle, what happens to the overflow?

Do you turn them down, hire more, or work with outside partners?

Just trying to understand how agencies like yours deal with it.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: Mercury Development and the hiring question
reverse_pitch.body:
Hey there,

Saw that Mercury Development has been handling overflow — exciting times.

Here's what I'm curious about: when you're scaling this fast, is it harder to find good dev talent or to manage the workload you already have?

I talk to a lot of agency owners and everyone has a different answer.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: what's your biggest bottleneck right now?
reverse_pitch.body:
Hi there,

I've been looking at custom builds agencies in Irvine, CA and Kingsmen Digital Ventures keeps coming up.

One question: what's the one thing slowing down your delivery the most right now — is it people, process, or something else entirely?

Genuinely curious.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: how do you handle the overflow at BairesDev?
reverse_pitch.body:
Hey there,

Random question — when BairesDev lands a big cloud/devops project and your large team is already stretched, what do you do?

I'm asking because I work with agencies on exactly this and everyone's approach is different.

Would love to hear how it works on your end.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: Infinum's mobile work — one question
reverse_pitch.body:
Hi there,

Stumbled across Infinum's work in mobile — really solid.

Quick question: do you keep all your dev work in-house, or do you ever bring in outside help when things get busy?

Just curious how a mid-size team handles the ups and downs.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: the custom builds capacity question
reverse_pitch.body:
Hey there,

I help custom builds agencies in Plano, TX with a specific problem — and I'm wondering if Agency Partner Interactive LL ever runs into it.

Here's the question: when your small team is at full capacity and a great project comes in, what do you do?

No pitch — just genuinely curious.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: curious about MOBIKASA's growth
reverse_pitch.body:
Hi there,

MOBIKASA seems to be handling overflow right now — always impressive to see.

One thing I'm curious about: as you grow, do you find it's easier to scale the team or to find reliable partners for the extra work?

Would love your take.

— SaJib

reverse_pitch.subject: a question about your small team
reverse_pitch.body:
Hey there,

I work with small team in team scaling and I've got a question that only someone running the show can answer.

What's harder right now — finding people who can actually deliver, or managing the projects you already have lined up?

Genuinely want to know.

— SaJib
